quick_facts.label: Core function
quick_facts.value: An Operation Theatre Supervisor combines administrative responsibility with technical work in an OT, under the respective OT medical officer-in-charge; a central-government hospital manual assigns the role staff deployment, technical-staff supervision, equipment readiness, supplies, safety and biomedical-waste responsibilities.

quick_facts.label: Typical setting
quick_facts.value: The role is based in hospital operating-theatre services and may cover routine and emergency OTs, including coordination of theatre sterile and supply functions and medical-gas readiness.

quick_facts.label: Career-entry pattern
quick_facts.value: This is commonly a progression role rather than a universal entry-level designation. For example, GMCH Chandigarh’s 2023 recruitment-rule proposal fills its Operation Theatre Supervisor post 100% by promotion from Senior Technician Anesthesia staff with two years of regular service.

quick_facts.label: Public-sector pay context
quick_facts.value: Pay is employer- and recruitment-rule-specific. In GMCH Chandigarh’s 2023 proposed rule, Operation Theatre Supervisor is a Group B, non-ministerial post at 7th CPC Pay Matrix Level 6 (₹35,400–₹1,12,400); this should not be treated as a national salary benchmark or an in-hand-pay figure.

sections.heading: What an Operation Theatre Supervisor does
paragraphs: The role is responsible for keeping theatre services operational as well as technically safe. A government hospital duty manual lists administrative responsibilities under the OT medical officer-in-charge, deployment of available technical staff across routine and emergency OTs, and supervision of technical staff working in the OTs.

The same manual assigns readiness and continuity tasks: maintaining anaesthetic and surgical equipment; maintaining inventory of drugs and consumables for anaesthetic equipment; ensuring the Theatre Sterile and Supply Unit and medical-gas pipeline/backup supplies function; and ensuring patient, fall and fire-safety measures plus biomedical-waste-policy compliance.
bullets: Plan and deploy OT technical staff for scheduled and emergency activity.
Supervise daily technical work and escalate issues through the OT clinical leadership structure.
Maintain equipment, consumable and gas-supply readiness so operations are not interrupted.
Lead practical compliance with sterile-supply, safety and biomedical-waste processes.

sections.heading: Entry routes in India
paragraphs: There is no single India-wide entry qualification or recruitment method evidenced by the sources for this exact title; requirements are set by the recruiting hospital or public employer. The strongest public-sector example located is GMCH Chandigarh’s 2023 proposed rule, where Operation Theatre Supervisor is a promotion post for Senior Technician Anesthesia personnel with two years of regular service.

A practical route is therefore to build foundational OT/anaesthesia-technology competence and clinical experience, then move through technician or senior-technician roles where the employer’s cadre structure provides a supervisory promotion path. GMCH’s associated senior-technician rule provides one example of a formal technical feeder level, while the supervisor rule identifies the next promotion step.
bullets: Complete the OT-technology or anaesthesia/OT training required by the particular employer or state recruitment notification.
Gain hands-on OT experience in a technician-level post, including equipment and perioperative workflow exposure.
Meet the relevant employer’s service-length, seniority, examination, departmental-promotion or direct-recruitment conditions; these are not uniform across India.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: The role charter makes operational leadership skills essential: roster and deployment coordination, supervision, record/inventory discipline, and communication with technical staff and OT medical leadership. It also requires practical judgement about uninterrupted availability of anaesthetic and surgical equipment, consumables and medical gases.

Patient-safety competence is equally central. The cited hospital manual explicitly includes patient and fall safety, fire safety and biomedical-waste policy, while an allied-health postgraduate programme describes supervisory OT work as managing staff, standards, records, inventory and human resources.
bullets: OT workflow coordination and staff supervision.
Equipment, medical-gas and sterile-supply readiness management.
Inventory control for theatre and anaesthesia consumables.
Infection-prevention, biomedical-waste, patient-safety and fire-safety practice.
Clear documentation, escalation and cross-team communication.

sections.heading: Workplace and working relationships
paragraphs: This is a hospital surgical-services role, working in and around operating theatres rather than an office-only supervisory post. The official duty manual places the supervisor under the respective OT medical officer-in-charge and assigns coverage of routine and emergency OTs, technical staff, theatre sterile-and-supply services, equipment and medical-gas systems.

Because the job is centred on uninterrupted OT function and safety controls, work can involve coordination across surgical, anaesthesia, sterile-supply, stores/maintenance and emergency-theatre activity. The exact reporting lines and shift arrangements depend on the hospital.
bullets: Routine and emergency operating theatres.
Theatre sterile and supply functions.
Anaesthetic and surgical equipment support.
Coordination with OT clinical leadership and technical staff.

sections.heading: Progression possibilities
paragraphs: A common internal progression pattern is from OT support or technician work into senior technical responsibility and then OT supervision, but the titles and qualifying service differ by employer. GMCH Chandigarh’s 2023 proposed structure explicitly identifies Senior Technician Anesthesia as the feeder grade for Operation Theatre Supervisor, requiring two years of regular service for promotion.

Further development can combine advanced anaesthesia/OT education with leadership responsibilities. Nitte’s Master in Anaesthesia & Operation Theatre Technology identifies clinical-supervisor/OT-manager work as an outcome and describes responsibilities including supervision of clinical staff, standards, records, inventory and human resources.
bullets: OT technician or anaesthesia/OT technical role.
Senior technician or comparable experienced technical role.
Operation Theatre Supervisor / OT Manager / clinical-supervisor responsibilities, subject to employer structure and eligibility.
Potential longer-term routes into education, research or specialised clinical-operations leadership with appropriate qualifications and experience.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Compensation varies substantially by employer type, state, cadre, allowances, experience and whether the role is public-sector or private-sector. As a dated public-sector reference only, GMCH Chandigarh’s 2023 proposed recruitment rule places Operation Theatre Supervisor at 7th CPC Pay Matrix Level 6, ₹35,400–₹1,12,400. That published matrix range is not a statement of take-home pay, a current vacancy offer or a nationwide market rate.
Applicants should use the pay level or consolidated salary stated in the specific recruitment notice and separately check allowances, contractual terms, shift/on-call expectations and deductions.
bullets: Use individual vacancy notices—not general online salary figures—to compare offers.
Distinguish pay-matrix basic pay from gross and in-hand pay.
Treat older recruitment-rule pay references as context, not as a current universal entitlement.
